schema.appDescription: Calculate VRS compensation — ex-gratia, gratuity, and leave encashment. Know the tax implications and net payment under Section 10(10C) exemption.

howToSteps.name: Enter your details
howToSteps.text: Fill in the input fields for the VRS Calculator. Smart defaults are pre-filled.

howToSteps.name: View instant results
howToSteps.text: Results update in real time — no submit button needed.

howToSteps.name: Explore breakdown
howToSteps.text: Open the year-by-year breakdown to see numbers over time.

howToSteps.name: Compare scenarios
howToSteps.text: Adjust inputs to compare scenarios and find the best option.

faq.q: What is VRS ex-gratia and how is it calculated?
faq.a: VRS ex-gratia = lower of: (a) 3 months' salary for each completed year of service, or (b) salary × remaining months of service. Formula A for 20 years: 3 × monthly salary × 20 = 60 months' salary. Formula B: salary × months remaining to 60. The lower of the two is the VRS payment.

VRS compensation is exempt from tax under Section 10(10C) up to ₹5 lakh (public sector) or as per Central Government scheme for other employees. The ₹5 lakh VRS exemption is combined for all years — you can claim it only once in a lifetime.

faq.q: Who is eligible for VRS in India?
faq.a: VRS is available to employees of central/state governments, PSUs, and companies with 50+ employees. Eligibility: completed 10 years of service OR attained 40 years of age (either criterion). The employee must apply voluntarily — compulsory separation is different from VRS.

examples.heading: ₹80,000 basic, 20 years service, 10 years remaining
examples.text: VRS Formula A: 3 × 80,000 × 20 = ₹48 lakh. Formula B: 80,000 × 120 months remaining = ₹96 lakh. Ex-gratia = ₹48 lakh (lower). Plus gratuity ₹9.23 lakh + leave encashment ₹3.69 lakh. Total VRS package ≈ ₹60.9 lakh.

examples.heading: Tax on VRS package
examples.text: ₹48 lakh ex-gratia has ₹5 lakh exemption under 10(10C). Taxable VRS = ₹43 lakh. At 30% bracket: tax = ₹13.32 lakh. Careful planning — taking VRS in a year with no other income minimises tax. Some spread VRS across financial years.
